_comment: Tunables for how the companion FEELS. Distances are in pixels, speeds in px/sec, intervals in seconds. 'personality' is the legacy 0..1 fallback; 'traits' are the live, drifting versions seeded from it. 'drift' controls how slowly the companion comes to reflect how you play. Tune all of this to change the vibe without touching code.
_follow_comment: The companion's comfortable distance TIGHTENS as the bond grows. Fresh, it keeps a wide berth (follow_near_low) — its own creature, barely seeming tied to you — and as you bond this shrinks to the snug at-your-side follow_near. That same distance is the follow deadzone, the trailing distance, and the 'near' bubble that feeds bond growth, so an unbonded companion is easy to be 'near' (just share the screen) and a bonded one wants you close. The viewport is 640x360 with the camera locked to the player, so ~180px is roughly the top/bottom edge: a fresh companion can roam almost to the screen edge before follow_far + the leash reel it back.
_wander_comment: Self-directed roaming — the independent, pre-bond life. Left alone the companion moseys to a spot, lingers, then after a pause picks somewhere new. wander_radius: how near a prop must be to be chosen as a destination (it investigates props it passes). roam_radius: how far an aimless amble strays when there's no prop to head for — kept just inside the screen so a fresh companion ranges wide without vanishing. roam_pause: [min,max] seconds of standing about between roams. roam_pause_bond_scale: pauses are multiplied toward this as bond->1, so a bonded companion ranges less. wander_linger: seconds it dwells once it arrives.
_bond_comment: The relationship itself (0..1), persistent across sessions. It starts at 'init' (a fresh companion is its own creature) and grows: grow_per_sec always while present, grow_per_sec_near extra while you stay within the (bond-scaled) comfort distance, grow_per_interaction on each shared examine. With these values an engaged player who explores alongside the companion reaches a full bond in roughly 5 minutes, and it is saved so a returning companion already knows you.
_balance_comment: How bond tips the wander-vs-follow contest. Self-wander scores wander_score_low at bond 0 (its own agenda) fading to wander_score_high at bond 1. Following scores follow_eager_low..follow_eager_high by bond, PLUS a distance leash (0 at the comfort distance ramping to follow_leash at follow_far) so it never lets you get truly far. follow_eager_low is 0 so a fresh companion has no pull to trail you at all — only the leash, near the screen edge, reels it in. A player-triggered look is fixed well above all of these.

traits._comment: init must match personality above so first-run feel is unchanged. min/max bound how far a trait can drift; drift_rate is how fast (per second toward target) — keep it small so change is felt, not seen.

drift._comment: warmup_seconds: watch this long before drifting at all. reference_* set what counts as 'a lot' of roaming / examining. targets: weighted blend of play signals (explore, together, engage) each trait drifts toward.
